job_statement: When I submit a health insurance claim, I want to track its status in real time, so I can stop wondering whether it was received and when I will be reimbursed.
When my medication is changed, I want to understand why it was changed and whether it is safe, so I can take my medicine with confidence instead of worry.

When I see a problem with my claim or medication in the portal, I want to take action directly (dispute, appeal, ask a question), so I do not have to leave the portal and start a separate phone process.

When I need to manage health administration for multiple people (children, elderly parent), I want a single dashboard with profile switching, so I can handle everything in one session without logging in and out.
When I use a digital health portal, I want the language to be plain and jargon-free, so I can understand what is happening without needing someone to translate for me.

When something changes in my health insurance (medication, coverage, claim status), I want to be notified proactively on my phone, so the information comes to me instead of me having to go looking for it.
When I feel overwhelmed by health administration, I want to feel that I am a competent person who can handle my own affairs, so I can maintain my sense of independence and self-worth.

When my sensitive health information (mental health, stigmatized conditions) is in a digital system, I want granular control over what is visible and to whom, so I can protect my privacy without losing access to the service.

When I am managing health administration for someone with dementia, I want to receive notifications and make changes on their behalf through formal authorization, so I can keep them safe without the system requiring their direct participation.

When I have a chronic condition and use the portal frequently, I want the system to recognize my usage pattern and prioritize my needs, so I feel acknowledged as someone who depends on the system more than the average user.

When I am new to managing my own health administration (recently widowed, recently retired), I want a safe practice environment where I can explore without consequences, so I can build confidence before using the real system.
When I choose a care provider, I want to understand the ranking criteria and filter by my specific condition, so I can make an informed choice rather than picking a name from an opaque list.

When the referral letter process involves me as an intermediary, I want the system to handle it automatically between my GP and VGZ, so I am not a bottleneck in a process that should be fully digital.
When I use a health portal, I want to see an audit log of who has accessed my data, so I can verify that my information is being handled appropriately.
